attacking_style: Not a natural attacker; works best as a steady climber within GC groups rather than launching long-range solo moves. Alpe d'Huez collapse (2018) showed inability to sustain breakaway efforts over extended HC climbs.
team_tactics: Positioned as protected GC rider within Jumbo-Visma's multi-leader strategy, but team quickly abandons GC support when early form concerns emerge (2021 Tour). Relies on team tempo control rather than personal aggression.
terrain_preference: Performs best on sustained climbs as part of elite GC groups rather than explosive finishes or solo efforts. Final climb positioning shows consistency when in form (matched Quintana/Masse 2018 Vuelta).
vulnerability: Extremely susceptible to illness/crashes derailing preparation and in-race form. Harsh weather stages appear particularly problematic. Lacks explosive acceleration for time bonuses, allowing rivals to gain margins on identical climbing performances.
key_patterns: Prone to pre-race/early-race form collapse requiring GC plan abandonment (2021 Tour, 2021 Dauphiné)
Illness and injury repeatedly compromise Grand Tour participation and performance (4 negative health-related signals)
Cannot sustain long solo efforts on HC climbs; fades dramatically when isolated (Alpe d'Huez collapse)
Loses GC positions via time bonuses despite matching climbers physically (2019 Tour fade to 3rd)
Career trajectory sharply negative post-2019 podium: multiple DNFs and form failures in 2020-2021
form_trajectory: negative: Sharp decline from 2019 Tour podium to consecutive abandonments and form collapses through 2020-2021. Team no longer investing GC protection.
